alert.summary: 5xx error rate on checkout-api exceeded 5% for 5m

metrics.pattern_description: Error rate stepped sharply from 0.2% to 8.7% at a single point in time; latency mildly elevated; CPU/memory nominal.

logs: ERROR checkout-api: NullPointerException in PaymentValidator.validate() at line 214
ERROR checkout-api: Uncaught exception while processing order 88213
INFO checkout-api: request_id=9f2c... status=500 duration_ms=210

ground_truth.root_cause: Deploy v2.14.3 (6 min before the alert) introduced a NullPointerException in PaymentValidator, causing checkout requests to fail with 500s.

metrics.pattern_description: Memory usage climbs linearly from 200MB to the 1.5GB container limit over ~25 minutes, then the pod is OOMKilled and restarts; pattern repeats.

logs: ERROR recommendation-service: OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
WARN recommendation-service: GC overhead limit exceeded
INFO kubelet: Killing container recommendation-service (OOMKilled)

ground_truth.root_cause: A steady, linear memory growth pattern ending in OOMKill points to a memory leak introduced in v5.2.0 (likely an unbounded in-memory cache added to the recommendation ranking module).

metrics.pattern_description: Active DB connections pinned at max pool size (50/50); queued query count rising; CPU on the DB proxy itself is low.

logs: ERROR orders-db-proxy: connection pool exhausted, timeout waiting for connection
ERROR orders-db-proxy: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms
WARN orders-db-proxy: slow query detected (duration=28s) on orders_history table

ground_truth.root_cause: A batch reporting job is running a 28s+ unindexed query against orders_history, holding connections open and starving the pool for normal order traffic.

alert.summary: payments-gateway seeing 100% failure calling external processor 'GlobalPay'

metrics.pattern_description: Outbound calls to GlobalPay time out at the 9s client timeout; internal service health (CPU/mem) is completely normal.

logs: ERROR payments-gateway: GlobalPayClient timeout after 9000ms calling https://api.globalpay.example/v1/charge
ERROR payments-gateway: circuit breaker OPEN for globalpay-client
INFO payments-gateway: internal health checks: OK

ground_truth.root_cause: Third-party payment processor GlobalPay is experiencing an outage on their end; payments-gateway itself is healthy but blocked on the dependency.

metrics.pattern_description: Disk usage climbing ~2%/hour over the last two days with no corresponding traffic growth.

logs: ERROR log-ingest-worker: No space left on device (os error 28)
ERROR log-ingest-worker: failed to write buffered batch to /var/spool/ingest
WARN log-ingest-worker: /var/log/ingest has grown to 41GB, rotation appears stalled

ground_truth.root_cause: Log rotation stopped working after a config change disabled the logrotate cron job, so old logs are never cleaned up and the disk is filling.

alert.summary: Spike in TLS handshake failures on api-gateway public endpoint

metrics.pattern_description: Failures begin abruptly at midnight UTC and affect 100% of external clients; internal service-to-service traffic unaffected.

logs: ERROR api-gateway: TLS handshake error: certificate has expired (x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid)
ERROR api-gateway: client rejected connection: certificate expired
INFO api-gateway: cert 'api.example.com' expiry was 2026-07-04T00:00:00Z

ground_truth.root_cause: The public TLS certificate for api.example.com expired at midnight; auto-renewal via the ACME client failed silently three days ago.

alert.summary: search-api p99 latency up 6x after feature flag rollout

metrics.pattern_description: Latency jumps immediately after a feature-flag change event; error rate barely moves, so this looks like a performance regression rather than a crash.

logs: INFO feature-flags: flag 'search_use_new_ranker' flipped to 100% rollout
WARN search-api: ranker model 'v3-experimental' request took 2950ms
INFO search-api: falling back to cached results after slow ranker response

ground_truth.root_cause: The 'search_use_new_ranker' feature flag was rolled out to 100% of traffic; the experimental ranker model is far slower than the legacy one and was not load-tested at full traffic.

metrics.pattern_description: Traffic volume climbing steadily over 30 minutes correlated with a marketing campaign launch; error rate and latency remain within normal bounds, autoscaler is adding pods.

logs: INFO auth-service: autoscaler added 4 new replicas (target CPU 70%)
INFO auth-service: request rate 12,400 req/min (baseline ~3,000 req/min)
INFO auth-service: no elevated error rate observed

ground_truth.root_cause: Legitimate traffic surge from a marketing campaign, not a failure. The autoscaler is already handling load correctly; error rate and latency remain healthy.

alert.summary: inventory-service in us-east-1b cannot reach primary DB in us-east-1a

metrics.pattern_description: 100% of requests routed through the 1b availability zone fail; 1a and 1c are unaffected, pointing to an AZ-local network issue rather than an application bug.

logs: ERROR inventory-service: connect timeout to db-primary.us-east-1a.internal from az us-east-1b
ERROR inventory-service: no route to host 10.4.12.9
WARN network: elevated packet loss reported between us-east-1a and us-east-1b

ground_truth.root_cause: A network partition between availability zones us-east-1a and us-east-1b is blocking cross-AZ traffic; this is an infrastructure-level fault outside the application.

metrics.pattern_description: Error rate jumps immediately following a deploy event; no gradual ramp, consistent with a bad release rather than resource exhaustion.

logs: ERROR pricing-service: KeyError: 'discount_tier' in PricingEngine.compute()
ERROR pricing-service: unhandled exception in /v1/price endpoint
INFO pricing-service: deploy v9.0.1 completed 4 minutes ago

ground_truth.root_cause: Deploy v9.0.1 removed a field ('discount_tier') that PricingEngine.compute() still depends on, causing KeyErrors on every pricing request.

alert.summary: home-feed-service cache hit rate dropped from 92% to 4%

metrics.pattern_description: Cache hit rate collapsed at the same moment a popular cache key expired, causing a thundering-herd of requests to hit the backend simultaneously; backend CPU spiking as a result.

logs: WARN home-feed-service: cache key 'feed:trending:global' expired, hit rate dropped to 4%
WARN home-feed-service: backend origin CPU at 88%, request queueing observed
INFO home-feed-service: 40,000 concurrent regeneration requests for the same key detected

ground_truth.root_cause: A high-traffic cache key ('feed:trending:global') expired without a lock/jitter strategy, causing a thundering-herd cache stampede that overloaded the backend.

alert.summary: notification-service cannot resolve email provider hostname

metrics.pattern_description: Failures are near-instant (fail fast) rather than timeouts, consistent with a resolution failure rather than a slow/unavailable endpoint.

logs: ERROR notification-service: dial tcp: lookup smtp.emailprovider.example: no such host
ERROR notification-service: DNS resolution failed for smtp.emailprovider.example
WARN notification-service: local DNS cache last refreshed 6h ago

ground_truth.root_cause: Stale/poisoned local DNS cache entries are causing lookups for the email provider hostname to fail; the provider itself is reachable.

alert.summary: order-processor consumer lag on 'orders.created' topic growing unbounded

metrics.pattern_description: Consumer lag rising linearly while consumer CPU stays low, meaning consumers are idle/stalled rather than overloaded.

logs: WARN order-processor: consumer group 'order-processor-group' lag = 184,320 messages and climbing
WARN order-processor: consumer thread pool utilization at 3%
INFO order-processor: no errors thrown, consumers appear idle

ground_truth.root_cause: Consumer count did not scale with a recent increase in order volume; too few consumer instances are running to keep up with the 'orders.created' topic.

alert.summary: billing-service getting 401s from internal ledger API

metrics.pattern_description: 100% of calls to the ledger API fail with 401 starting exactly at a scheduled secret-rotation time; no other symptoms.

logs: ERROR billing-service: ledger-api responded 401 Unauthorized
ERROR billing-service: API key 'billing-svc-key-v3' rejected
INFO secrets-manager: scheduled rotation of 'billing-svc-key' completed 5 minutes ago

ground_truth.root_cause: A scheduled secret rotation updated 'billing-svc-key' in the secrets manager, but billing-service's cached credential was not refreshed, so it is still presenting the old, now-invalid key.

alert.summary: recommendation-service CPU throttling detected on 3 nodes

metrics.pattern_description: CPU pinned near 100% with visible cgroup throttling; another workload was co-scheduled on the same nodes moments before the alert (noisy neighbor).

logs: WARN kubelet: container recommendation-service throttled, cpu.cfs_throttled_time increasing rapidly
INFO scheduler: batch-ml-training-job co-scheduled onto node-17, node-18, node-19
WARN recommendation-service: request latency p99 degraded to 1500ms

ground_truth.root_cause: A batch ML training job was scheduled onto the same nodes as recommendation-service, causing CPU contention ('noisy neighbor') and throttling.
